study.specs_notes: Three launcher specs form a layered dependency chain: Service Configuration (leaf) → Launching System Processes → Stopping System Processes. Service Configuration defines a YAML schema for 3 service endpoints (portal:8080, api:8081, optimizer:8082) with strict validation, defaults, and a --config CLI flag. Launching spec defines a short-lived orchestrator that spawns 3 detached processes (spectacular-portal/api/optimizer via PATH), writes a daemon state file (~/.spectacular/daemon.json with PIDs/ports/timestamp), health-checks all 3 concurrently (GET /health with poll+timeout), and enforces all-or-nothing startup with SIGTERM cleanup on failure. Uses exclusive file creation to prevent concurrent launches. Stopping spec defines a stop subcommand that reads daemon.json, sends SIGTERM to all 3 PIDs (best-effort, order-independent), waits with aggregate timeout, and deletes daemon.json only on full success. Idempotent — missing daemon.json exits 0. Stale PIDs treated as already-stopped. The reconciliation commit (69c7373) added Service Configuration as an integration point to the stopping spec, clarifying that stop reads ports from daemon.json rather than config directly.
study.code_notes: Launcher is spec-only — no Go code, no go.mod, no implementation yet. Has Makefile (test/lint/cloc targets) and CLAUDE.md referencing Cobra via context7. Peer domain patterns: api/ is Go (module spectacular/api, go 1.21.6, deps: coder/websocket + goccy/go-yaml, structure: cmd/server/main.go + internal/{domain,server,translator,workspace}). Optimizer is Python (pydantic/pyyaml/loguru/dspy/websockets). Portal is TypeScript/Next.js. Protocols are spec-only (ws1/ws2). Shared conventions: each domain has Makefile wrapping language tools, CLAUDE.md with context7 refs, golangci-lint for Go. Root Makefile lists DOMAINS=api launcher optimizer portal prompts. No go.work file — Go domains managed independently. Launcher will follow api/ patterns: Go module, cmd/ entry point, internal/ packages, Cobra CLI (per CLAUDE.md), detached process spawning, YAML config via goccy/go-yaml or similar.
study.packages_notes: Launcher will be a Go module (go 1.21.6, matching api/). Key dependencies needed: spf13/cobra for CLI (per launcher CLAUDE.md context7 ref), goccy/go-yaml for YAML config parsing (same as api/). Standard library covers the rest: os/exec for process spawning, os/signal for SIGTERM, net/http for health checks, encoding/json for daemon state file, time for timeouts/polling, path/filepath for file paths, syscall for process groups and detaching. No external health check or process management libraries needed — the specs describe straightforward poll-based health checking and os.Process management. golangci-lint for linting (matches api/ convention).
